source.title: Otitis media with effusion in under 12s — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England, United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE paediatric, audiology, and ENT adaptation
source.population: Children under 12 years with suspected or confirmed otitis media with effusion and their parents or carers.
source.setting: Primary, paediatric, audiology, and ENT recognition, assessment, reassessment, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for paediatric OME-associated hearing, communication, balance, mouth-breathing and adenoid context; otoscopy, hearing testing, tympanometry, reassessment, and post-grommet review. It does not support acute otitis media, adults, automatic treatment, or a procedure decision.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.7 — concerns, impact, recognition, and formal assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

items.previous_wording: follow-up documented if arranged by clinician

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.7 — concerns, impact, recognition, and formal assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

items.rationale: The exact paediatric OME recognition section directly reviews mouth breathing, snoring, nasal obstruction, adenoid history, hearing, communication, balance, functional impact, ENT examination, hearing testing, and tympanometry; it supplies partial evidence only and does not diagnose adenoid hypertrophy.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.
